description_long: This is a classic wooden barrel with a cylindrical, slightly bulging body and flat, closed ends. The exterior is constructed from vertical wooden staves, bound tightly by two wide, black metal hoops—one near the top and one near the bottom. The wood surface displays a natural, weathered grain with subtle variations in tone, giving it an authentic, rustic appearance. The metal bands are matte and feature visible rivets, reinforcing the structure and adding to its durability. There are no visible handles, spouts, or decorative markings, and the top is fully closed, indicating it is not currently open for filling or emptying. This type of barrel is commonly found in urban settings such as alleys, storage yards, or near workshops, serving as a container or decorative element.
description: A weathered wooden barrel with black metal hoops and a closed top, featuring a rustic, utilitarian design. The surface is textured with wood grain and reinforced by matte metal bands.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full curvature of the barrel, with both metal hoops and the wood grain clearly visible.
description_view_1: Slightly rotated view highlighting the side curvature and the seamless connection of the metal bands around the barrel.
description_view_2: Another angled view emphasizing the vertical wood staves and the even spacing of the metal hoops.
description_view_3: Rear view with similar features, showing the continuity of the wood texture and the matte finish of the metal bands.
